results.matched_effect_cluster: Prompt-injection as role confusion + role boundary
results.justification: Direct twin — explicitly analyses representational role-hijack and role-boundary enforcement; MAJLIS-GATE is one specific instantiation.
Defensive token insertion + role isolation directly covers status-axis mask aspect.
Defensive tokens prevent injection via attention-level isolation.
4-layer structured-prompt-formatting + semantic-output-validation covers dual-axis defense pattern.
Privilege hierarchy via role tokens is direct status-axis precursor; MAJLIS-GATE extends to per-token mask.
Many-Tier hierarchy with conflictable-instruction anchors covers tier-graded status-axis.
Token-importance-driven sparse attention is functionally similar at the mechanism level.
All-context-untrusted + least-privilege benchmarks justify exactly MAJLIS-GATE's status-axis design.
